# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

class ApplicationMailer < ActionMailer::Base
  default from: ENV.fetch("MAIL_FROM", "no-reply@example.com")
  layout "mailer"
end

class SendgridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  def self.sendgrid_client
    @sendgrid_client ||= begin
      api_key = ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"]
      raise "SENDGRID_API_KEY is not set" if api_key.nil? || api_key.empty?

      SendGrid::API.new(api_key: api_key).client
    end
  end

  def self.deliver_via_sendgrid(mail)
    personalization = SendGrid::Personalization.new
    mail.to.each { |email| personalization.add_to(SendGrid::Email.new(email: email)) }
    mail.cc.to_a.each { |email| personalization.add_cc(SendGrid::Email.new(email: email)) }
    mail.bcc.to_a.each { |email| personalization.add_bcc(SendGrid::Email.new(email: email)) }

    from_email = SendGrid::Email.new(email: Array(mail.from).first)
    subject = mail.subject.to_s

    content = []
    mail.body.parts.each do |part|
      mime_type = part.mime_type
      next unless %w[text/plain text/html].include?(mime_type)

      content << SendGrid::Content.new(type: mime_type, value: part.body.decoded)
    end

    if content.empty?
      content << SendGrid::Content.new(type: "text/plain", value: mail.body.decoded)
    end

    message = SendGrid::Mail.new(from_email, subject, personalization, content.first)
    content[1..].to_a.each { |c| message.add_content(c) }

    if mail.reply_to.present?
      message.reply_to = SendGrid::Email.new(email: Array(mail.reply_to).first)
    end

    if mail.attachments.present?
      mail.attachments.each do |attachment|
        message.add_attachment(
          SendGrid::Attachment.new(
            content: Base64.strict_encode64(attachment.body.decoded),
            filename: attachment.filename.to_s,
            type: attachment.mime_type,
            disposition: "attachment"
          )
        )
      end
    end

    response = sendgrid_client.mail._("send").post(request_body: message.to_json)

    unless response.status_code.to_i.between?(200, 299)
      raise "SendGrid delivery failed with status #{response.status_code}: #{response.body}"
    end

    response
  end

  def mail(headers = {}, &block)
    message = super

    if Rails.env.development? || Rails.env.production?
      message.delivery_method { self.class.deliver_via_sendgrid(message) }
    end

    message
  end
end

class UserMailer < SendgridMailer
  def welcome_email(user)
    @user = user
    mail(to: user.email, subject: "Welcome")
  end
end

Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :sendgrid
  config.action_mailer.smtp_settings = {
    address: "smtp.sendgrid.net",
    port: 587,
    authentication: :plain,
    user_name: "apikey",
    password: ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"],
    enable_starttls_auto: true
  }
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = {
    host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST", "localhost"),
    protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "http")
  }
end
